zh.Fornham All Saints, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可福恩哈姆 哥吉拉 沙智

zh.Holme upon Spalding Moor, Yorkshire, Eng: 英國越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Spalding於1425年出生在英國素福爾可法爾納姆時，他的父親James Spalding41歲，母親Joan Palmer25歲。他於1439年與Philippa Tomlinson在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他去世於1485年在他的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.Holme upon Spalding Moor, Yorkshire, Eng: 英国越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Spalding于1425年出生在英国素福爾可法爾納姆时，他的父亲James Spalding41岁，母亲Joan Palmer25岁。他于1439年与Philippa Tomlinson在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他去世于1485年在他的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Spalding
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Angus): variant of   Spaulding  .

History: The surname was taken to Scotland in the 13th century by Radulphus de Spalding. His descendants prospered, and the name is still common in Scotland. Early American Spaldings include Thomas Spalding, born in Frederica, GA, in 1774, who introduced sea-island cotton in GA, and the physician Lyman Spalding, born in Cornish, NH, in 1775, who founded US Pharmacopoeia.

variants.name: Spaulding
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire) and English (Cambridgeshire and Norfolk): habitational name from Spalding (Lincolnshire), from Old English Spaldas, the name of an Anglian tribe who settled chiefly in the fen-lands of Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire, + the groupname suffix -ingas. Compare Spafford .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Spalding was born in 1425, in Farnham, Suffolk, England, his father, James Spalding, was 41 and his mother, Joan Palmer, was 25. He married Philippa Tomlinson in 1439, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1485, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Spalding est né en 1425, à Farnham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, James Spalding, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Joan Palmer, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Philippa Tomlinson en 1439, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1485, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Spalding 1425 in Farnham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Spalding 41 und seine Mutter Joan Palmer 25. Er hat 1439 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Philippa Tomlinson geheiratet. Er starb 1485 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Spalding è nato il nel 1425 nel Farnham, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, James Spalding, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Joan Palmer, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Philippa Tomlinson nel 1439, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1485, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Spalding(이)가 1425년 영국 수폴크 패른함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Spalding(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Palmer(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1439년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Philippa Tomlinson(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1485년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Spalding nasceu em 1425, em Farnham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, James Spalding, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Joan Palmer, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Philippa Tomlinson em 1439, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 1485, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Spalding родился в 1425 г. в местности фарнам, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени James Spalding было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Joan Palmer, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Philippa Tomlinson в 1439 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1485 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Spalding nació en 1425, en Farnham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, James Spalding, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Joan Palmer, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Philippa Tomlinson en 1439, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en 1485, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años.
